Deploy

  • Documentation site
RGC Circuit Simulator
  • Quick start
  • View page source

Quick start

This section describes how to run the application. For the full window layout and where each control lives, see User interface. For plots that mirror the heatmap and LN code, see Examples and plots. For theory, see Conceptual overview and Model equations.

Installation

Requirements: Python 3.10+ (recommended), NumPy, SciPy, Numba, ModernGL, Dear PyGui, colour-science, Pillow, scikit-image, matplotlib, pyglm.

python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate    # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py

The main window opens with a 2D heatmap view by default. Use the View → Mode combo to switch to 3D Stack for the Signal Flow Column view.

Running the simulator

  • Stimulus is updated every frame from the current stimulus type and parameters (spot, grating, etc.). Time advances with the main loop (variable \(\Delta t\)).

  • Pause is not implemented; the simulation runs continuously while the window is open.

  • Export: In the Stats tab (right panel), use Export data to save a screenshot (PNG), layer stats (CSV), or layer grids (NPY).

For a description of the interface (panels, 2D/3D views, connectivity, etc.), see User interface.
